summary: Do not ship: mixed trusted/untrusted PRs bypass the executor’s branch-ownership boundary and permit a destructive rebuild.

findings.title: Blocker: a trusted target bypasses base-independent branch ownership
findings.body: The scan deliberately retains same-repository PRs for other bases because they also occupy the shared head ref, but this guard rejects them only when `trusted === null`. A trusted writer PR targeting `main` can coexist with an untrusted PR from the same head targeting another base; the trusted PR then wins selection, and a DIRTY/BEHIND decision force-pushes that shared ref. This rewrites the untrusted PR’s head without considering its reviews or other human state, contradicting the plan’s explicit base-independent ownership invariant. W11d covers only an untrusted other-base PR by itself, so it misses this composition.
findings.recommendation: Model target identity and exclusive head ownership separately. If any untrusted same-repository PR occupies the head, prohibit force-push and other unsafe mutations regardless of whether a trusted target PR also exists; perform only explicitly safe revocations and exit nonzero. Add a mixed fixture containing a stale trusted main PR plus an untrusted different-base PR and require zero push/create/arm before rebuilding the fixed-test RED/GREEN records.

next_steps: Fix the mixed-occupancy ownership guard and add the missing regression witness.
Regenerate the machine-owned RED/GREEN verification records without changing existing tests.
